videos.title: Are supercars POINTLESS? Corvette Z06 v Rivian R1T: DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
Are supercars POINTLESS? Corvette Z06 v Rivian R1T: DRAG RACE Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-1104

Mat’s flown over to the USA for an American car showdown! 

He’s got his hands on an all-new Rivian R1T Quad Motor, and he’s going up against a Corvette Z06! 

It’s a petrol vs electric head-to-head, but does this colossal all-electric pick-up truck have what it takes to bring down one of the most iconic American sports cars? Let’s take a look at the stats… 

Starting with the Rivian, it’s equipped with four electric motors (one for each wheel), and these combine to produce 1,025hp and 1,624Nm of torque! It comes with launch control, but it also comes with a hefty weight, tipping the scales at 3,170kg! New, it costs from $119,000. 

Then we come to the Corvette. It’s powered by a 5.5-litre naturally-aspirated V8, and this creates 670hp and 625Nm. All this power is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed dual-clutch gearbox, and it weighs in at almost half the weight of the Rivian - 1,685kg! A new one of these will have a price tag of around $120,000. 

So they both cost around the same, but that’s about where the similarities between these end! So, which do you think will snatch the win? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE! 

Thanks to everyone who helped set up this race:

videos.title: I Tested 24 AI Models on OpenClaw
videos.summary_source_text:
I Tested 24 AI Models on OpenClaw ✨ Join my community and chat about OpenClaw 🦞 https://mrc.fm/cmc

I tested 24 AI models on OpenClaw to find out which one has the best personality, tool calling and speed now that Anthropic killed Claude Max subscriptions. I used Claude Code to spin up sub agents and run every model on exactly the same health AI prompt, blind tested with code names so there's no bias. I compared Ollama, OpenRouter, OpenAI Codex, xAI, Anthropic API and even self hosted models, broke down the costs and ranked them all in a tier list from D to S. The results genuinely surprised me and the winner isn't what you'd expect.

videos.title: Why I’d NEVER buy a £300,000 Bentley...
videos.summary_source_text:
Why I’d NEVER buy a £300,000 Bentley... Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Car-Vertical

Welcome to the carwow review of the new Bentley Bentayga Speed! 

We’re putting this all-new, luxurious SUV to the test to see if it can still hold its own against the flashiest rivals on the planet, such as the Lamborghini Urus, Ferrari Purosangue, or the ultra-opulent Rolls-Royce Cullinan. 

While the Urus is a track-focused weapon and the Cullinan is a literal palace on wheels, the Bentayga Speed tries to occupy the sweet spot in the middle. But does it offer enough for you to choose it over its rivals? Let's take a closer look...

At first glance, you might think not much has changed because it shares the same iconic body, grille, and bonnet as the previous model. However, the Speed hallmarks appear upon closer inspection. To differentiate it from the standard V8, Bentley has swapped out the traditional chrome for a sleek, blacked-out trim across the exterior. This model also marks the first time you can spec massive 23” alloy wheels that fill the arches perfectly. Our test car even features the optional carbon fibre side skirts, a chunky roof spoiler, and a vocal Akrapovič exhaust system!

Bentley continues to set the bar for cabin quality, and every single element you touch feels expensive - we couldn't even find any wonky stitching! The cabin is a masterclass in textures, blending high-end Leather and Alcantara with stunning wood trim. While the digital dials look great and modern, Bentley has wisely kept physical dials for the climate control, making it far easier to use on the move than its touch-screen-only rivals. The infotainment system is also intuitive enough to ensure the tech never gets in the way of the luxury.

The biggest news for the new Bentayga Speed is under the bonnet, where the legendary 6-litre W12 has been replaced by a downsized 4-litre V8. Interestingly, Bentley has avoided adding any hybrid power here, keeping it as a pure internal combustion engine producing 650hp and 850Nm of torque. Even though it has lost four cylinders, it has not lost its punch. It is now lighter over the front end, making it feel more agile than ever, while the Akrapovič exhaust ensures it sounds just as mean as the old twelve-cylinder.

All of this luxury comes with a high price tag. While the standard Bentayga starts at £172,600, the Speed model featured in this video comes in at a cool £290,295 thanks to a staggering £70,000 worth of options! But is it the smart choice over its close rivals? Stick around and see for yourself!

videos.summary_source_text:
Sam Altman on Building the Future of AI AI is advancing faster than most people realize. In this OpenAI Forum conversation, Sam Altman joins Josh Achiam and Adrien Ecoffet to talk about what’s coming next.

They discuss the pace of progress toward more capable AI systems, what these tools could unlock—from scientific breakthroughs to new ways of building companies—and the challenges society needs to prepare for. The conversation also looks at who gets access to AI, how to make sure the benefits are widely shared, and what it will take for institutions and individuals to keep up. Is this the ultimate electric SUV showdown? Mat’s back, and this time he’s brought the heavy hitters of the luxury EV world.

In this video, we’re pitting the established BMW iX against its newest, most formidable rivals. We have the boxy and brilliant Kia EV9, its sleek and sophisticated sibling, the Hyundai Ioniq 9, the ultra-minimalist Polestar 3, and the safety-focused Volvo EX90.

Which of these high-tech EVs offers the best range, the most luxury, and the best value for your money? Let’s find out!

The Contenders - Specs for the cars in this video:

- BMW iX: 109kWh battery, 403 miles range,  £75,405, 544hp (Dual Motor)

- Polestar 3: 107kWh battery, 438 miles range, £69,990, 300hp (Single Motor)

- Volvo EX90: 107kWh battery, 375 miles range, £73,160, 408hp (Dual Motor)

- Hyundai Ioniq 9: 106kWh battery, 372 miles range, £64,995, 427hp (Dual Motor)

- Kia EV9: 96kWh battery, 313 miles range, £66,035, 385hp (Dual Motor)

So what’s in this review?
- Design: From the bold BMW grille to the Ioniq’s "Aerosthetic" curves.
- Interior & Tech: Which cabin feels the most premium, and which infotainment system actually works?
- Practicality: We test the 7-seater capacity of the Kia, Hyundai, and Volvo versus the 5-seat luxury of the Polestar and BMW.
- Driving: Does a 2.5-tonne SUV have any right to be fast? (Spoiler: Yes).
- The Verdict: Which one should you buy, avoid, or lease?

So which EV SUV do you think will come out on top? Drop your guess in the comments, then join Mat for this group test!

videos.title: This New AI Might Be the Most Useful One Yet: CoWork
videos.summary_source_text:
This New AI Might Be the Most Useful One Yet: CoWork Abacus just released CoWork, and this might be one of the most useful AI desktop tools we’ve seen in a while. Instead of acting like another chatbot, CoWork is built to take messy folders full of receipts, PDFs, spreadsheets, logs, transcripts, Jira tickets, and compliance docs, then turn all of that into finished work people can actually use. In the demos, it audits expenses, rebuilds incident timelines, answers giant compliance forms with citations, analyzes supplier and pricing risk, repurposes podcast transcripts into content packages, and turns product research into PRD-ready output.

If this category keeps improving, AI tools start looking much more like real digital workers.
